recommended_for: Simple deterministic work, single-subsystem changes, clear write sets, runnable tests
performance_notes: Excellent speed-to-cost ratio. Best for straightforward implementation tasks with clear requirements. May struggle with ambiguous specs or complex architectural decisions.
notes: Default for most implementation tasks. Optimized for speed and cost.

recommended_for: Default for most implementation work. Higher-complexity tasks, cross-cutting changes, full-stack wiring, multiple coordinated files
performance_notes: Strong balance of capability and cost. Handles multi-file changes and cross-cutting concerns well. Good architectural understanding for medium-complexity tasks.
notes: Balanced capability and cost. Use when low-tier fails or task spans multiple subsystems.

recommended_for: Complex reasoning tasks requiring deep code understanding. Use sparingly.
performance_notes: Excellent for complex architectural reasoning and deep code analysis. Slower but more thorough. Strong at understanding implicit requirements and edge cases.
notes: High cost. Requires explicit user authorization unless task genuinely cannot be split.

recommended_for: Highest-complexity tasks. Explicit user request/authorization only.
performance_notes: Top-tier capability for most challenging tasks. Best at novel problem-solving and complex system design. Premium cost justifies use only for truly difficult work.
notes: Premium tier. Use only when gpt-5.3-codex is insufficient.

recommended_for: Documentation, commit messages, human-like writing style. Not optimized for code.
performance_notes: Optimized for natural language generation. Best for documentation, explanations, and user-facing content. Coding ability sufficient but not specialized.
notes: Use for docs and copy, not implementation. Plain and human-sounding output.

recommended_for: Large context requirements. Explicit user authorization only.
performance_notes: Extended context window for large codebases. Slower due to context processing but maintains coherence across many files. Use when task requires understanding extensive context.
notes: Extended context variant. Use only when context window is the limiting factor.

recommended_for: Most capable Claude model. Use for highest-complexity orchestration and planning.
performance_notes: Exceptional reasoning and planning capability. Best for architectural decisions and complex problem decomposition. Very slow and expensive - reserve for orchestration and high-level design.
notes: Premium tier. Excellent for planning and architecture decisions, not typical delegation.

recommended_for: Balanced Claude model for general-purpose work.
performance_notes: Well-balanced Claude model. Strong code generation with good reasoning. More expensive than GPT medium tier but offers different thinking style that can help with stuck problems.
notes: Good balance of capability and cost for Claude-based workflows.

recommended_for: Previous generation Sonnet. Use 4.6 instead unless specific compatibility needed.
performance_notes: Previous generation Sonnet. Slightly less capable than 4.6 but still strong. Use 4.6 for new work unless you need consistency with existing 4.5-based outputs.
notes: Consider upgrading to claude-sonnet-4-6 for latest improvements. EOL end of 2026.

recommended_for: Fast, cost-effective Claude model for simple tasks.
performance_notes: Fastest Claude model with good accuracy for simple tasks. More expensive than GPT low tier but offers Claude's different approach. Good when GPT mini fails on a simple task.
notes: Fastest Claude model. Good for simple, well-defined tasks.
